revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 建神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[美馬郡]][[つるぎ町]]半田字逢坂62 |位置= |祭神= [[素盞嗚命]] |社格= 旧村社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''建神社'''（たてじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[つるぎ町]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 歴史 == 創祀年は不詳。権現さんや権現はんの愛称で親しまれている。往古は半田山の天王という地にあったとする説がある<ref>{{cite web|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-5-14}}</ref>。
式内社である建神社の論社のひとつである。
== 祭神 == * [[素盞嗚命]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島線]]「[[阿波半田駅]]」より徒歩で約10分。
== 脚注 == <references />
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たてしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:つるぎ町の歴史]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 建神社 |画像= [[File:Tate-jinja (Tsurugi).jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}{{ill|Mima District|ja|美馬郡}}{{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}半田字逢坂62 |位置= |祭神= {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}} |社格= Former Village Shrine |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= }}
'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.
== References == <references />
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsurugi, Tokushima]] [[Category:History of Tsurugi, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[ja:建神社]]

== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”

== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.

== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.
== References == <references />

== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.

According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.

{{神社 |名称= 建神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image= [[File:Tate-jinja (Tsurugi).jpg|280px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}{{ill|Mima District|ja|美馬郡}}{{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}半田字逢坂62 |位置= | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity= {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type= Former Village Shrine |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }}
'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.
== References == <references />

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.
== References == <references />

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.

{{神社 |名称= 建神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image= [[File:Tate-jinja (Tsurugi).jpg|280px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}{{ill|Mima District|ja|美馬郡}}{{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}半田字逢坂62 |位置= | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity= {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type= Former Village Shrine |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }}
'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.
== References == <references />

{{神社 |名称= 建神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image= [[File:Tate-jinja (Tsurugi).jpg|280px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location= {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}{{ill|Mima District|ja|美馬郡}}{{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}半田字逢坂62 |位置= | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity= {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type= Former Village Shrine |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }}
'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 建神社 |画像= [[File:Tate-jinja (Tsurugi).jpg|280px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[美馬郡]][[つるぎ町]]半田字逢坂62 |位置= |祭神= [[素盞嗚命]] |社格= 旧村社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''建神社'''（たてじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[つるぎ町]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 歴史 == 創祀年は不詳。権現さんや権現はんの愛称で親しまれている。往古は半田山の天王という地にあったとする説がある<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>。
式内社である建神社の論社のひとつである。
== 祭神 == * [[素盞嗚命]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島線]]「[[阿波半田駅]]」より徒歩で約10分。
== 脚注 == <references />
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たてしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:つるぎ町の建築物]] [[Category:つるぎ町の歴史]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tsurugi, Tokushima|Tsurugi, Tokushima]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]].
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Awa-Handa Station|Awa-Handa Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]].
== References == <references />

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== References == <references />

{{draft categories| }}

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tsurugi, Tokushima|Tsurugi, Tokushima]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]].
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Awa-Handa Station|Awa-Handa Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]].
== References == <references />

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tsurugi, Tokushima|Tsurugi, Tokushima]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]].
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|ja|つるぎ町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|ja|阿波半田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== References == <references />
{{shinto-stub}}

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tsurugi, Tokushima|Tsurugi, Tokushima]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]].
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Awa-Handa Station|Awa-Handa Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]].

'''Tate Jinja''' (建神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tsurugi, Tokushima|en|Tsurugi, Tokushima|WD=Q1206005|lt=Tsurugi, Tokushima}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}.
== History == The year of its foundation is unknown. It is affectionately called Gongen-san or Gongen-han. According to one theory, it was once located in a place called Tenno in Handayama in ancient times.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/tate2_title.htm|title=建神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-14}}</ref>
It is one of the candidate shrines identified as the Shikinaisha “Tate Jinja.”
== deity == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== Access == * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Awa-Handa Station|en|Awa-Handa Station|WD=Q4829609|lt=Awa-Handa Station}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|en|Tokushima Line|WD=Q899310|lt=Tokushima Line}} of {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku}}.
